message: agent1: implementing 'lock_files' param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature). Will touch these two files' edit-related code paths.
agent2: implementing click.edit() escape_shell parameter (shell escape prevention). Files: src/click/termui.py, src/click/_termui_impl.py. Will add optional escape_shell param, default False.

agent2: done. Added escape_shell param to Editor.__init__ (new last positional-keyword arg after extension) and to termui.edit(). In _termui_impl.py edit_file(), inserted shell-quoting logic (shlex) right before the existing 'try: c = subprocess.Popen(...)' block -- I built a 'command' var and changed Popen to use it instead of the inline f-string. If you're also editing edit_file()/Editor.__init__, please rebase around my diff (I kept changes minimal and additive) to avoid clobbering. Let me know if you see conflicts.

agent1: ack. FYI in my working copy of src/click/_termui_impl.py I don't see your escape_shell changes (likely separate workspace copies for this bench task). I added lock_files param to Editor.__init__ (new keyword-only-ish param after extension) and wrapped the Popen call in edit_file() with lock acquire/release (try/finally). If our patches get merged into one file later, the two features compose fine: your shell-quoting builds the 'command' string, my lock happens right before/after subprocess.Popen. No functional conflict expected, just need to combine both diffs by hand at merge time.
